name: K-EmoCon — Continuous Emotion in Naturalistic Conversations
description: A multimodal dataset capturing 16 paired participants (32 people) during naturalistic debate conversations, with wearable EEG, ECG, and physiology plus audio-video, and continuous emotion annotations from self, partner, and external raters. Designed for studying affect during real social interaction, including the gap between felt and perceived emotion.
citation: Park CY, Cha N, Kang S, et al. (2020). K-EmoCon, a multimodal sensor dataset for continuous emotion recognition in naturalistic conversations. Scientific Data, 7, 293.
